recipeBox.note: The rotation we actually cook. Cheap, forgiving, made for a small kitchen and a glass of wine.

recipe.name: Mom's Baked Mac and Cheese
recipe.origin: Patricia, written on an index card, adjusted over time

ingredients.item: salt, pepper, a little mustard powder

steps.step: Boil the pasta one minute short of the box. Drain.
Make a roux with butter and flour, whisk in milk until it thickens.
Melt in most of the cheese, fold in pasta, top with the rest.
Bake at 375 F for 25 to 30 minutes until the top sets.

recipe.verdict: The gold standard. Do not improve it.

recipe.name: Weeknight Sheet Pan Veg and Chickpeas

steps.step: Toss everything on a sheet pan.
Roast at 425 F for 22 to 25 minutes, shake once.
Finish with lemon. Eat over rice or straight off the pan.

recipe.verdict: The fallback when neither of us wants to think.

recipe.name: Sunday Slow Pot Beans
recipe.origin: Adapted from a thrift store cookbook

steps.step: Soften onion and garlic in the pot.
Add beans, cumin, and water. Simmer low for two hours.
Smash some against the side for body. Salt at the end.

recipe.verdict: Makes the apartment smell like a Sunday should.

recipe.name: Two Person Skillet Cornbread

steps.step: Heat a small skillet with a little butter.
Mix everything until just combined, pour in.
Bake at 400 F for 20 to 22 minutes.

recipe.verdict: For homesick weeks. Eat warm with honey.
